target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $126.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Palantir Technologies is a public-stage AI and software company currently valued at $400B+. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir itself) and eight private frontier-tech peers in the AI and software sector, including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, Databricks, and Waymo.

The peer-median method was applied across five same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables. The median valuation among this set is $183.0B, with a range spanning $126.0B (Waymo) to $300.0B (OpenAI). This method reflects Palantir's positioning relative to other large-scale AI and software infrastructure players, though it captures only a single valuation approach given data constraints.

The triangulated analysis, derived from the peer-median method, supports a valuation range of $155.6B (low) to $210.4B (high), with a median estimate of $183.0B. This range sits 45–54% below Palantir's current public market valuation of $400B+. Key uncertainties include: reliance on a single valuation method; significant variance within the peer set ($126.0B–$300.0B); and the difficulty of precise comparable selection given heterogeneity in business models, growth trajectories, and capital structures across frontier-tech firms. The current market price substantially exceeds peer-derived fair value, suggesting either company-specific premium drivers or elevated market pricing in the AI sector.
